channel.title: Decarbonarma
channel.description: Clear, no-nonsense analysis of UK home decarbonisation - heat pumps, solar, EVs, batteries and the grid.

item.title: Putin's Accidental Climate Policy
item.description: How the invasion of Ukraine and the collapse of Russian gas supply drove Europe's clean energy transition further and faster than three decades of climate policy — and where that argument breaks down.

item.title: 2,700 Heat Deaths, One Summer: Why the Heat Pump That Cools Is No Longer Optional
item.description: Sky News reported the toll from this summer's heatwaves passing 2,000. The official Met Office/Imperial figure is over 2,700. We look at why most heat pumps can't cool properly, why the ones that can (A2W) do it badly, and what actually worked at Rose Cottage.

item.title: Solar Becomes Law in 2027: Will New Homes Still Get a Resale Premium?
item.description: From March 2027 every new English home needs solar covering 40% of its footprint. Once it's the law, not a luxury, does the resale premium survive — or does it just move somewhere else?

item.title: Britain's Grid Keeps Getting Margin Notices: Why Security of Supply Needs More Than Wind
item.description: Three heatwave-driven Electricity Margin Notices in one summer, low wind, and French interconnectors unable to bail Britain out. The case for treating grid security of supply as a national security question, not just a decarbonisation one.

item.title: Air-to-Air Heat Pumps: Has the British Summer Started Needing Air-Con?
item.description: We counted every day above 25C at Heathrow for the last five years. The British summer is quietly turning into an air-con season — and an air-to-air heat pump cooling on free solar surplus is the neatest answer.

item.title: Wiring an EV Charger with Solar & a Battery: The CT-Clamp Trick
item.description: Add an EV charger to a home with solar and a battery and the two can end up fighting — the battery drains itself to feed the car. The fix is a Henley block and getting the CT clamps in the right order. Here's how it works, in plain English.

item.title: UK Wind: Status H1 2026
item.description: Where UK wind power stands in mid-2026 — onshore and offshore capacity and output, what's under construction, the record AR7 auction, floating wind, and the elephant in the room: the billions spent turning turbines off.

item.title: Which EV Charger Should You Buy? An Interactive Decision Guide
item.description: Don't buy the wrong EV charger. A plain-English decision flow that walks you from 'should I go electric?' to exactly which home charging setup (A, B or C) fits your driveway, mileage and tariff.

item.title: The Beginner's Guide to Air Source Heat Pumps
item.description: Ground source, air-to-water, air-to-air — three very different machines all called 'heat pumps'. Which one is simple, which one needs a lot of plumbing, and why an air-to-air pump is basically air conditioning.

item.title: Daikin X Series vs Samsung EHS Quint: Do Either Change the Game?
item.description: Daikin's X Series and Samsung's EHS Quint both blend air-to-air and air-to-water heating in one unit. What's real, what's marketing, and what it means for buyers.

item.title: Nuclear Power in the UK: Where It Stands in 2026
item.description: Britain's ageing reactors are winding down as a new generation of giant plants and small modular reactors gears up. The full 2026 picture, with capacity charts back to 1980.

item.title: 3 Mad EV Lease Deals Right Now (and Their Real Running Cost)
item.description: Three genuinely cheap electric car lease deals in the UK — from £137/month — plus the bit the brochures skip: what they really cost to run once you charge overnight or off your own solar.

item.title: H1 2026: Six Months of Metered Solar & Battery Data From Rose Cottage
item.description: Six months, four solar arrays, one battery: the full metered H1 2026 breakdown from Rose Cottage — North, South, Garage and the original Sonnen array compared, plus battery cycling, grid position and the real financial return.

item.title: Air-to-Water Heat Pumps Are Dead
item.description: Air-to-water heat pumps dominate UK grants, but air-to-air is cheaper, simpler and works year-round. The case for a quiet revolution, and the honest caveats.

item.title: Air-to-Air Heat Pump + Sunamp: My Setup Instead of a Gas Boiler
item.description: Why I heated my home with an air-to-air heat pump and a Sunamp heat battery instead of a gas boiler and cylinder or an air-to-water system — the real costs, the grant catch, and who it suits. As featured on Skill Builder.

item.title: The Top 5 Home EV Chargers (2026)
item.description: The five best home EV chargers for 2026, ranked on tech and integration with Intelligent Octopus Go, the cheap-charging tariff. The good, bad and ugly of each.

item.title: The Last Good Gas Boiler: How to Buy, Size and Run It to Use the Least Gas Possible
item.description: Heat pumps are the right long-term answer, but if gas is still your only option: how to size, buy and run a boiler to use the least gas possible.

item.title: The Cheapest New Car in Britain?
item.description: A new electric SUV is leasing for £138.55 a month, cheaper than a city car. What you really pay after tax, insurance and charging, and whether it's any good.

item.title: The State of Decarbonisation: What the World Has Done in the First Half of 2026
item.description: Renewables overtook coal globally in 2025. A review of the world's biggest decarbonisation efforts from January to May 2026, and what comes next.

item.title: Rose Cottage: From Gas to Nearly Zero
item.description: How one homeowner combined fabric upgrades, a four-layer heating cascade, solar PV and smart energy management to cut gas use by 60% in a season, with the data.

item.title: The Netherlands is Running Out of Grid. The UK is Next
item.description: 7,300 Dutch households are stuck in a decade-long grid connection queue. What the Netherlands' electrification crunch means for Britain's own grid plans.

item.title: Nature as Climate Engine
item.description: Reintroducing a single predator can reshape a landscape and pull carbon back into the soil. How trophic cascades make nature one of the most powerful climate tools.

item.title: Home Energy Optimiser
item.description: A free tool that works out the cheapest way to run your home's actual heating mix, picks the right tariff, and ranks every upgrade by payback period.

item.title: 5 Home Battery Storage Solutions You Need to Consider
item.description: Five UK home battery storage systems compared cheapest to premium, with real specs, prices and VPP ratings, from budget DIY kits to fully managed smart systems.

item.title: Does Brand Matter? What 600+ Real Heat Pumps Actually Tell Us
item.description: Vaillant vs Mitsubishi vs Samsung vs Daikin: what 639 real, metered heat pumps from HeatpumpMonitor.org actually say about whether brand affects performance.

item.title: The Triple Barrel: Eddi + Sunamp + Tepeo ZEB — 50.7 GWh of Scotland's Wasted Wind
item.description: Adding a Tepeo Zero Emission Boiler to an Eddi-and-Sunamp setup pushes Scotland's curtailed wind absorption even higher. Part 2 of the Wasted Wind series.

item.title: The Real Cost of Going Electric: EV vs Diesel
item.description: A diesel Mercedes GLE and Mini against their electric equivalents, same household, same mileage. The real cost comparison, with no fudged assumptions.

item.title: Can an EV Pay for Itself? The Frontera & the Free-Car Test
item.description: Can switching from petrol to a £138.55-a-month EV lease pay for itself in saved fuel, tax, MOT and tyres? We ran the numbers on the Vauxhall Frontera.

item.title: You Decarbonised Your Home. Should You Have Bought the Stocks Instead?
item.description: £30,000 on solar, a battery and a heat pump is a bet on the green economy. We compare that spend against what the same money would have returned in green stocks.

item.title: Is a Heat Pump Worth It in the UK? An Honest 2026 Answer
item.description: No sales pitch. A homeowner who fitted his own heat pump runs the real 2026 numbers — grant, running costs vs gas, and when it genuinely isn't worth it.

item.title: SCOP 6 Has Already Been Broken — By a Tyrolean Startup You've Never Heard Of
item.description: A six-year-old Austrian startup, Lambda Wärmepumpen, has posted a WPZ-certified SCOP of 6.06, breaking a barrier the industry wasn't expecting yet.

item.title: HeatpumpMonitor.org — What the Data Actually Says
item.description: What 639 real UK heat pumps, tracked by HeatpumpMonitor.org, reveal about efficiency, flow temperatures and what good installation design actually looks like.

item.title: COP 4.8 in Scotland: What Barry Sharp's NIBE S1155 PVT Tells Us About the Future of Home Heating
item.description: A NIBE S1155 PVT ground source heat pump using roof-mounted PVT panels instead of a borehole has delivered a COP of 4.8 in Scotland over 954 days of data.

item.title: Drill, Baby, Drill? The Case For and Against UK Domestic Fossil Fuel Production
item.description: New North Sea licences and fracking are now banned in the UK. The case for and against domestic fossil fuel production, and why the tension hasn't gone away.

item.title: Samsung EHS Quint Deep Dive: One Box, Five Modes, Free Hot Water From Cooling
item.description: A full technical breakdown of Samsung's EHS Quint — the first residential heat pump to do air-to-air and air-to-water from one outdoor unit, with heat recovery that makes free hot water while it cools. Specs, the grant angle, and the honest caveats.

item.title: The 70-Reactor Question: Can SMRs Replace Britain's Ageing Gas Fleet?
item.description: The UK signed its first Small Modular Reactor contract in 2026. Replacing the whole gas fleet would need roughly 70 reactors and £175 billion, here's the maths.

item.title: Are Solar Panels Worth It in the UK in 2026? Honest Cost & Payback
item.description: No sales pitch. The real 2026 cost of solar (and a battery), honest payback maths by region, and when it's genuinely not worth it — from a homeowner who fitted it.

item.title: Buy Cheap or Go Premium? The Real Quality Differences in Solar
item.description: Budget panels vs premium panels, microinverters, and the battery brands everyone argues about: the real quality differences that matter in a solar system.

item.title: Shell Bought the Future of Home Energy — Then Put It in a Drawer
item.description: Shell paid around €500 million for home battery startup Sonnen in 2019, then tried to sell it five years later. What went wrong with the Green Acquisition.

item.title: The Heat Battery That Replaced Our Hot Water Tank in Four Hours
item.description: When Rose Cottage's hot water cylinder failed, a Sunamp Thermino 300e replaced it in four hours and has run on free solar surplus ever since. How it works.

item.title: Superfoil + Solar Roof Retrofit: What It Really Cost & Saved
item.description: A homeowner re-roofs with Superfoil insulation and solar and gets his whole-home energy bill down to £20/month — the real cost, the metered output, and whether multifoil is worth it. As featured on Skill Builder.

item.title: Can the Grid Handle It? The Maths of Electrifying Every UK Home and Car
item.description: 23 million gas boilers, 37 million EVs, 100% clean power by 2030: we ran the maths on whether the UK grid can actually handle full electrification.

item.title: The Wind Is Blowing. The Turbines Are Switched Off. And You're Paying For It.
item.description: Britain pays wind farms to switch off while firing up gas elsewhere to cover the gap, costing every household £34 in 2025. The scandal, and the decade-long fix.

item.title: Why Home Wind Power Doesn’t Add Up — The Cube Law
item.description: A turbine on your roof seems like the natural twin to solar panels. The cube law explains why home wind almost never works, in plain English.
